title: Cross-Encoder, Late-Interaction, Diversity, and Reranking · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for cross-encoder, late-interaction, diversity, and reranking and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, safe abstention, explicit uncertainty, and named native-model, vector-store, provider, load, security, recovery, or production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for budgeted retrieve-and-rerank pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debuggin…
objectives: Separate high-recall candidate retrieval, bounded expensive reranking, final context selection, and their independent cutoffs.
Apply a query-document cross-encoder with batching, truncation, calibration, model-card limits, and target relevance evaluation.
Explain token-level late interaction, index cost, maximum similarity, query/document encoding, and when it earns complexity.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

options.text: Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

For diagnose the hidden failure, run one convenient Rag Reranker Budget Fallback query, omit corpus and version identity, relevance judgments, tenant policy, changed documents, abstention, citations, failure and recovery, then accept a plausible answer as RAG proof.

steps.title: RAG and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Rag Reranker Budget Fallback
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Rag Reranker Budget Fallback: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz RAG engineering team handles case 5484 during a multilingual support escalation. Build deterministic Python evidence to budget bi-encoder candidates, cross-encoder or late-interaction reranking, quality, and latency; retain corpus, document, chunk, model, index, prompt, query, and evaluation version identity; then double corpus size and candidate depth while preserving the latency, memory, and cost envelope. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Set candidate, token, batch, time, memory, retry, and fallback budgets and test quality under timeout and partial failure.

content.title: Evidence defense
